error_map.hint: thrive-learning username/password is missing or invalid; re-run pm credentials add thrive-learning
thrive-learning rate limit exceeded; the connector will retry with backoff

conformance.reason: this stream's real endpoint lives on the current Thrive Public API (https://public.api.learn.link/rest/v1/activities per docs.thrivelearning.com), a different host than the bundle's base.url (which points at the legacy connector's own simplified api.thrivelearning.com endpoint); the engine resolves an http(s)-prefixed stream.path as an ALREADY-ABSOLUTE request target (connsdk.Requester.resolveURL) and never substitutes the conformance replay server's origin for it, so a declarative fixture replay would dial the real internet instead of the test double (the same shape as squarespace's contacts stream / defillama's stablecoins stream). Read correctness is proven by this stream's own fixtures/streams/activities/{page_1,page_2}.json shape (matches the published OpenAPI PaginatedActivities envelope) and by the same schema-projection path every other stream uses; only the dynamic (live-replay) checks are skipped, not the static schema/fixture-shape checks.

absolute-URL real-API stream on a different host than base.url; see the activities stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation (same connsdk.Requester.resolveURL limitation). Read correctness is proven by fixtures/streams/contents_v1/{page_1,page_2}.json (matches the published OpenAPI PaginatedResponse/Content envelope).

absolute-URL real-API stream on a different host than base.url; see the activities stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation. This endpoint's own documented response is a BARE array (no results/pagination envelope, unlike most other v1 endpoints) so records.path is '.'; read correctness is proven by fixtures/streams/learning_completions/{page_1,page_2}.json (matches the published OpenAPI Completion[] shape).

absolute-URL real-API stream on a different host than base.url; see the activities stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation. This endpoint's own documented response is a BARE array (no results/pagination envelope); read correctness is proven by fixtures/streams/assignments/{page_1,page_2}.json (matches the published OpenAPI Assignment[] shape).

absolute-URL real-API stream on a different host than base.url (see the activities stream's conformance.reason); additionally a fan_out stream whose id-listing preliminary GET is itself an absolute-URL request the replay harness cannot intercept. Read correctness of the fan_out shape itself is covered by the engine's own fan_out unit tests (internal/connectors/engine/read_test.go); this stream's own record shape is proven by fixtures/streams/assignment_enrolments/page_1.json (matches the published OpenAPI Enrolment[] shape) and records_match_schema.

absolute-URL real-API stream on a different host than base.url; see the activities stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation. Read correctness is proven by fixtures/streams/audiences/{page_1,page_2}.json (matches the published OpenAPI AudienceStub/Pagination envelope).

absolute-URL real-API fan_out stream on a different host than base.url; see the assignment_enrolments stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation. This resource has no per-record globally-unique id field (AudienceMember's own userId is only unique within one audience's listing); [audience_id, userId] is the genuine composite key, matching this migration's ip2whois [domain, role] composite-key precedent (conventions.md ledger item 11).

absolute-URL real-API fan_out stream on a different host than base.url; see the assignment_enrolments stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation. This resource has no per-record globally-unique id field either (same composite-key reasoning as audience_members); the endpoint's own documented response is an unpaginated bare array.

absolute-URL real-API stream on a different host than base.url; see the activities stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation. The documented /tags list endpoint takes no page/pagination parameters at all (unlike every other v1 list endpoint) so pagination.type is none; read correctness is proven by fixtures/streams/tags/page_1.json (matches the published OpenAPI Tag[]/results envelope).

absolute-URL real-API stream on a different host than base.url; see the activities stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation. Read correctness is proven by fixtures/streams/cpd_categories/{page_1,page_2}.json (matches the published OpenAPI PaginatedCPDCategories envelope). Primary key is categoryId, not id -- this resource's own documented identifier field name, unlike every other v1 resource.

absolute-URL real-API stream on a different host than base.url; see the activities stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation. Read correctness is proven by fixtures/streams/cpd_entries/{page_1,page_2}.json (matches the published OpenAPI PaginatedCPDEntries envelope). Primary key is logEntryId, this resource's own documented identifier field name.

absolute-URL real-API stream on a different host than base.url; see the activities stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation. Read correctness is proven by fixtures/streams/cpd_requirements/{page_1,page_2}.json (matches the published OpenAPI PaginatedCPDRequirements envelope). Primary key is audienceRequirementId, this resource's own documented identifier field name.

absolute-URL real-API stream on a different host than base.url; see the activities stream's conformance.reason for the full explanation. This is a small, fixed, unpaginated configuration list (the documented response is a bare array of {name, isEnabled, value} with no page/pagination parameters at all); read correctness is proven by fixtures/streams/skill_levels/page_1.json (matches the published OpenAPI response shape).
